item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 175 replies — 271.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 175 • Updated: 2026-07-12 18:51 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469146
nigger just do what foids do get a comfy wfh hr, middle manager, project manager, customer liason, customer service or other phone operator job.

62469410
We shouldn't have to go to some corporation just to get an income.

62469809
>>62449961
Not fucked at all. Just lie on your resume, they lie about the job description, it's all bullshit. I was a NEET for 10 years after high school and now I'm on track to retire at 50. You really only need 20 years of working to make it, if you're smart with your money.

62470572
>>62457986
>>62457969
neither answered because they made that shit up lol

62470630
>>62449961
Literally just lie. Lie, lie, and lie some more. Accept that you can only live a lie because no one normal will accept and understand your situation. Stay disciplined and keep chipping away at things daily until you find an opportunity. There's a Chinese proverb that "Heaven never seals off all exits".

62470681
>>62449961
>almost 40
>NEET
>not 7 figures yet, probably not even in 6 figure hell
Enjoy being part of the permanent underclass

62470736
You have two options, just lie on your resume, or apply to a job where they take anyone. The latter is typically telemarketers and collections agencies and shit like that. If you lie on your resume you will need someone who can pose as references for you. Reach out to friends and family to fake references. You may have to get a dedicated phone number for them.

62470784
>>62470736
>>62458848
I'm also lying on my resume, this is the thread I made 62470742. How feasible is the lie I made?

62470870
>>62451269
man, giving up "for a while" is like 6 months of not working, maybe a year. If you couldnt get a job, any job, in 20 years and hold it down, you are below nigger tier. 60 IQ crackhead noghs can manage to show up to their job at Wendy's often enough to not get fired

62470964
>>62449961
>>62449979
go volunteer. go uber/lyft/doordash get SOMETHING on your damn resume. what's your story?? why were you good without a job up until 40 and now you suddenly need one? how were you surviving? you need to have a somewhat hopeful/convincing story as to why you've had a change of heart to participate in society starting now

62470981
>>62468936
actuary is a highly competitive field especially among new grads. you're going to need some type of experience and the first 2-3 exams passed (ideally in quick succession to show you can pass them). i would get a job at an insurance company doing literally anything, claims adjuster, call rep, whatever while you're passing the first exams and then apply to everything like crazy because you'll be non-traditional
>Passing the first round of exams would be easy
if it's so easy then go do them

62470991
>>62465843
sounds like you're already financially set, why do you want an office job? no doubt you could do one though.
>>62469809
>>62470630
the majority of employers try to verify employment somehow, especially if they suspect you of lying. what exactly should op put down as a lie?

62470995
>>62470991
Do you think my lie will work? >>62470742

62471054
>>62470991
>the majority of employers
he just needs to find the one that doesn't
that's why he needs to apply everywhere

62471058
>>62471054
I've applied to hundreds of jobs through indeed and none have replied back, do I just forget about these big job agencies and just apply to company websites directly?

62471268
>>62470991
A trick that worked for me is that I went to a temp agency, you don't need a resume for that, they hire anyone. You show up any day you want and maybe work that day, if something comes up they give you a brief description of the job and you can accept or decline. You work that day and get paid that day. All kinds of various random jobs, construction, picking up garbage around a mall, sorting the metal out of demolition debris, sweeping floors etc. You get all kinds of things to put on a resume and the variety makes it seem like you've been working for years. Work there for a few weeks or months and then lie about how long you've been doing those jobs to fill the gap. If the employer calls them about your employment history there, they will know who you are and vouch for you. Also the freelance nature of it makes it easier to ease into working after being NEET for so long. The downside is that you sometimes work with literal hobos or tweakers.

62471310
>>62470991
>the majority of employers try to verify employment somehow
No, they don't. Even if they have an HR bimbo who is the bosses ex-wife or daughter she would be too lazy to call every applicants references and past jobs. You live in crab bucket and deserve to go back to faggot.

62471672
>>62470981
>if it's so easy then go do them
the first exams are basically undergrad math and stats, the only exam that would have stuff im not immediately familiar with is the finance and business one. I recognize that the ones after that are much harder but they are not worth doing unless you already have a job. It's like 1k per exam it's a lot if it's all for nothing if they won't even look at me. Everybody will have these exams.
>i would get a job at an insurance company doing literally anything,
even that is easier said then done for a pseudo NEET

62471680
>>62449961
are you a felon? can you pass a drug test? are you able to lift something 50 pounds?

62471769
>>62471680
Can you kiss my ass?

62471839
>looking for your first job at 40
You can look forward to a comfortable retirement at 130

62471846

62471853
>>62471846
I try using Google ai and it tells me everything from saving 15k to 60k per year.

62472133

Fucking 40 and you argue with your mom about your internet connection? I'm sorry, I mean HER internet. You might not want to conform to the rules of society like some retarded hippie, but even the hippie found a way to make money.

Seriously KYS. That's the best advice you can get here, you fucking baby. Want some sympathy and fake advice? Go cry on Reddit.

62472153
>>62449961
Work in medical field, work grunt white collar job and be a yes man, do a niche trade or work 2 brain dead easy low paying jobs and basically collect money for existing. Many jobs like that in California, btw nothing is beneath you and you're fucking lazy, stop making threads here and go do something, but you're trolling anyways. You only get to your spot in life because your insane ego fooled you into not doing anything

62473146
>>62468936
If you have a master's there should already be some bs job that hires you.
I know 2 people in your shoes and they did exactly that, some bs gov job or companies who contract with military.
The Master degree tells them you're not retarded and can learn the job but has no relevance to your schooling

62473155
>>62449961
I bet you were one of those neet posters and now mommy and daddy are getting to old or are dead/dying and you suddenly have to get that wagie job you were making fun of

62473189
>>62473155
>t. wagie
Kek. I am neet and also 40

62473197
>>62473155
Seething wagie, i live toyally of NEETbux + crypto. Tick tock, wagefuck.

62473199
>>62473155
Being a slave isn't a good thing. You're supposed to get out of a bad situation not build a personality around it and defend like a vegan feminist.

item.title: I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking. — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-12 19:17 UTC

OP

I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking.

I'm an ugly beta male in my mid 30s. My social life has been non-existent for my entire adult life. I missed out on any socialising during university and my 20s and afterwards. All of human society is built for normies and ugly beta males like me are seen as shit.

My job is pointless and WFH. I'm overpaid and any sideways move will lead to worse living conditions and commuting. I'm too ugly and non-normie to ever be promoted.

Culturally, my time has passed. I have recently gained the habit of pining over playing 90s and 00s video games, and then being bored to death when actually playing them. I played video games a lot in my youth, then barely any in my 20s, and a bit more in recent years. But it's all pointless.

I have produced nothing. I have no skills. I am a viewer of lives. I missed out on the web 2.0 boom, the crypto boom, every boom, every trend. The things I do, reading and going to the gym and walking, feel like procrastination. I have never taken anything to its full conclusion. I have never put sustained effort into anything.

Early replies (first 3)

62473079
how do i unsubscribe from your blog faggot

62473080
>>62473075
You sound like 90% of people.
You realize 90% of people have nothing but work in their lives right.

62473081
Many such cases.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

>>62473075
Ever thought about going to a doctor? You don't have to take the pills they offer you, you know. But just to know what's wrong with you. It could be as simple as ADHD.

>>62473075

62473190

Britain.

>>62473086

I have nothing mentally wrong with me. I am rationally assessing things.

62473203

Oh well in that case if you feel like your life isn't meaningful you could always perform domestic terror attacks against the demons that killed your society.
Just make sure to commit suicide if ever caught.

62473215

Your issue is that you've put a "cap" on your growth. You believe thats it for you. AI exists as not only a teacher, but a self-improvement tool and you have not even taken the steps to learn why. This is why you are stuck
>You've forsaken self-improvement, ACTUAL self improvement and thus are stagnant. Try something new, like any self-improver does.
Get out of your closed loop cycle and grow anon. No matter the age you can get anywhere if you progress towards it. I myself am a HDI (High Demand Individual) and I've played games and not graduated college. But I have kept my researches on the cutting edge (not taught in college, but will be) industries and so these "new" things coming out to the normies I have already self-improved on. It has taken my 8/10 skillset and transformed it to 8000/10 skillset. This isn't truly a exaggeration and I am currently self improving by joining companies that want me to run a department, then use their resources to improve my skillset further while benefitting said company. I am using them to grow stronger, yet here you are, whining because you cannot find new things to do. If that is what 90% of the population is like, then naturally I will sought out for my capabilities.
>It is only over for you anon when you throw in the towel. Learn something new that you feel motivated to do more of, then master it
If you try something new and you do not like it, then move onto something new. Its literally that shrimple

I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). >>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 105 replies — 67.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-07-12 17:56 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>>62458801
Powerwash 100 driveways for $100 each 10 times.

62470191
love your attitude op so i've come to bring you knowledge that yall may already know. i'm almost at the 6 figure table.

1. live with your parents, you losers are probably already doing this. with minimal living expenses you can invest more. also allows you to drop the emergency fund which again lets you invest more.

2. be young, youth is an advantage for compounding interest. a huge advantage.

3. get a job, yes a wage slave. obviously a better job will allow you to invest more. it won't be forever. the sooner you hit the rpg grind irl the sooner you can spend the rest of your life eating hot pockets and playing vidia.

4. invest in growth etf's. your crypto is fools gold. yes the stock market can crash but it always bounces back even if it takes years to do it. and you all are young enough to ride out the bad years. once you get old, or have enough then sell your growth etf and get dividend etfs and live the good life.

5. you can't just invest in safe etf's but you have to place them in the right location.

5a) 401k up to employer match, this is the best because its free money for you but the investments are usually not the best. if you have the option get the ROTH 401k not the normal one if the company offers it. for a normal 401k what you put in is tax differed, when you draw from it at 59 1/2 then you pay taxes on it.

5b) ROTH ira is you own personal retirement account. you pay taxes on what you put in but no taxes on what grows inside or when you pull your money out. 59 1/2 also. has a limit on how much you can invest into it each year so max it.

5c) health savings account, hsa. what you contribute can be deducted from your taxes, what grows in it not taxed and when you spend it on a medical expense its not taxed. if you save your bills you can reimburse yourself at a later date once the account grows huge. at 65 you can just withdraw from the account as if it was income.

im just some old fag trying to help my younger brothers.

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY

62472755

62472818
>>62461988
Everest didn’t randomly integrate Stellar — they built on it deliberately because Stellar is positioned to become the compliant public blockchain backbone for the tokenized economy, and Everest is engineering the regulated on-ramps/wallets/payments layer to capture it. Here’s the maximalist, everything-aligns thesis:
1. Strategic Positioning at the Perfect Intersection
• Everest = MiCA-licensed (EU passport), compliant custody, payments, fiat on/off-ramps, and now native Stellar wallet generation (BIP39 in EverWallet via SCF Kickstart Award).
• Stellar = Fast, cheap, compliance-native (clawbacks, authorized trustlines, etc.), with massive institutional validation via DTCC.
• The Kickstart Award isn’t just funding — it’s SDF officially stamping Everest as a key builder. This opens doors to deeper integration, more grants, co-marketing, and priority support as Stellar scales.
Everest becomes the regulated European gateway for Stellar flows: EU users/institutions access DTCC-tokenized assets (stocks, ETFs, Treasuries), stablecoins, and cross-border payments seamlessly through EverWallet.

62472822
>>62472818
DTCC connecting its tokenization service to Stellar (DTC assets live H1 2027) is one of the biggest TradFi-crypto bridges ever. We’re talking infrastructure for quadrillions in annual settlement volume potentially touching a public chain.
• Tokenized RWAs on Stellar need compliant wallets, custody, transfers, and EU distribution.
• Everest’s MiCA license + Stellar-native wallet = plug-and-play solution for European participants. No need for institutions to build everything themselves.
• Result: Explosive volume on Stellar higher XLM demand (fees, liquidity, bridges) + massive utility for Everest’s platform.
This isn’t payments-only anymore. It’s Everest sitting at the intersection of Wall Street tokenization and EU-regulated access.
3. Tokenized Economy Flywheel
• MiCA Advantage: Everest is already authorized for custody, transfers, etc. As MiCA matures and tokenized securities/stablecoins boom in Europe, Everest white-labels solutions for merchants, fintechs, and institutions.
• Wallet + Payments Moat: EverWallet with seamless Stellar (plus BTC/SOL roadmap) becomes a multi-chain compliant hub. Users generate Stellar addresses easily, hold tokenized assets, send remittances, swap, earn — all regulated.
• Network Effects: More assets on Stellar (DTCC + others) more users need compliant interfaces Everest captures fees, custody spreads, and ecosystem partnerships. SCF validation accelerates this.

62472827
>>62472822
• Stellar TVL and volume surge from DTCC flows XLM utility and price re-rate significantly (institutional adoption narrative goes parabolic).
• Everest, as an early regulated builder with SDF backing, sees user growth, revenue from services, and potential token ($ID) or valuation upside as the “Stellar-compliant layer in Europe.”
• Multi-chain expansion (already hinting BTC/SOL) positions them beyond one network while Stellar remains the core for real-world compliant assets.
• Timing: 2026–2027 window is when pilots go live. Everest’s Kickstart work is shipping now, giving them first-mover polish in wallets/UI.
Why They Chose Stellar “For a Reason”: Low costs + speed for payments/remittances, built-in compliance primitives perfect for regulated tokenized securities, and now direct DTCC pipeline. Ethereum is great for DeFi but heavier/costlier for high-volume settlement. Stellar is the efficient, institutional-friendly public chain.
In the super scenario: DTCC/Stellar becomes the standard for tokenized securities. Everest rides it as the go-to EU-compliant wallet/custody/payments layer. XLM reprices for infrastructure status. Everest scales into a major fintech player in the tokenized economy, with strong revenue, user base, and ecosystem gravity.
This is the aligned, non-random bet: regulation + infrastructure + timing + grants + institutional tailwinds. High conviction setup if the DTCC integration delivers.

>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.
